brand.name: Cinch Traps
brand.wordmark: CINCH (stamped block letters on yellow field with galvanized-steel trap illustration) — FIXED asset, never redesigned
brand.location: 10140 SW Allen Blvd Suite A, Beaverton, OR 97005
brand.hours: Orders fulfilled within 24 hours on business days
brand.palette_guidance: CINCH yellow #FFCC00 (dominant logo field color, 7726px sample) + black #111111 + galvanized-steel silver #BBC1C4 + white; warm off-white surfaces #F6F5F0/#FBFAF6; darkened gold #8A6D00 only for small links on white. BANS: yellow text on white/light surfaces (1.36:1 contrast fail — yellow is a FIELD/ACCENT color only); no invented hues (no coral, teal, blue, purple); no washed-out pastel gradients. Black-on-yellow is the signature CTA/badge pairing (13.9:1 AAA).
brand.voice: Plain-spoken, confident, folksy-but-credible. Heritage craftsman tone: 'tried-and-true', evidence-led, zero hype, lightly wry about the critters, respectful of the customer's frustration.
brand.identity_belief: The proven original — 116 years of handmade Oregon steel beats every gimmick on the shelf.
brand.visual_metaphor: Stamped galvanized steel + the underground tunnel: a thin yellow tunnel-line motif that runs beneath the surface of the page like a mole run, surfacing at section seams; stamp-style badges evoke the steel 'CINCH' stamp.

content.headline: The Original Mole & Gopher Trap. Tried-and-True Since 1909.
content.subhead: Ranked #1 by five universities and the USDA. Handmade from American galvanized steel. Lifetime warranty. Free shipping on trap orders.
content.cta_secondary: Mole or Gopher? Find your trap
content.imagery: Full-bleed golden-hour photograph: healthy green lawn/field at sunset, warm low sun flare, a homeowner walking with a dog in the middle distance (no face detail). Dark bottom-weighted scrim so white/yellow-on-dark type passes AAA. Grounded, real, warm Americana — NOT stocky.

content.style: single black band, items separated by thin yellow ticks, Oswald-style condensed uppercase, stamp-badge feel

content.headline: Mole or Gopher? Read the Mound.

left_card.title: MOLE
left_card.body: Round cone of fine, fluffy soil — like a tiny volcano.

right_card.title: GOPHER
right_card.body: Fan or horseshoe-shaped mound with a plugged hole to one side.

content.imagery: Side-by-side comparison photos of a mole mound vs a gopher mound in real lawn/soil, shot straight-on, matched framing.
content.note: Trap size is chosen by tunnel diameter — S / M / L.

content.eyebrow: FREE SHIPPING ON TRAP ORDERS · CONTINENTAL US
content.headline: Kits Include Everything You Need.
content.imagery: Clean studio product photography of galvanized-steel scissor traps and boxed kits on light warm-gray wells (client's real SKU library look: raw steel trap with yellow tag; yellow CINCH kit boxes).

content.eyebrow: NO POISON. NO GUESSWORK. NO TOUCHING THE ANIMAL.
content.headline: Set It in 4 Simple Steps.

steps.label: Open it with the tunnel tool
Set two traps facing opposite directions

content.style: quiet section — generous negative space, numbered steps in stamped yellow squares, thin diagram-line illustrations in black, no photography needed

content.headline: Handmade in Oregon From American Galvanized Steel — Since 1909.
content.body: If it's not stamped CINCH, it's not the original. Built on Don Sprague's legacy of innovation, problem-solving, and a genuine passion for helping growers succeed.
content.usda_quote: “CINCH Trap was the all-around best performer.” — USDA National Wildlife Research Center
content.imagery: Dark section: macro detail of galvanized steel trap mechanism / brushed-steel texture, warm workshop light, yellow stamped accents. Neutral placeholder area for an archival founder photo — NEVER a generated person.

content.headline: The Only Product That Has Worked.

quotes.quote: I caught the Mole King NEXT DAY after receiving and setting “small” traps. Been after the dude for 6 months and tried everything.
quotes.author: Verified customer

quotes.quote: I have tried them all. I gave up on the stores and searched for the traps online. I found Cinch Traps and they truly work.
quotes.author: Sam Street

quotes.quote: We use them in our yard here in east Texas — these gophers don't stand a chance!
quotes.author: Jim from Texas

quotes.quote: The quality and effectiveness is amazing. It's definitely a product I would recommend to everyone I know.
quotes.author: Rich Olson

content.style: quote wall on warm off-white; oversized yellow quotation marks; NO star icons, NO rating counts anywhere

content.signup_headline: Trapping Tips From the Oregon Workshop.
content.signup_sub: Seasonal reminders and field-tested advice. No spam.
content.signup_style: solid CINCH yellow band, black text and black input outline

footer.address: Cinch Traps LLC · 10140 SW Allen Blvd Suite A, Beaverton, OR 97005
columns: Shop (Mole Traps, Gopher Traps, Accessories, Apparel)
Learn (How It Works, Mole & Gopher Info, FAQs, Reviews)
Company (About, Legacy of Don Sprague, Contact)
footer.style: black footer, white text, yellow accents, wordmark as plain type

customer_asks.ask: Lead with warm, golden-hour outdoor lifestyle photography (EXAMPLE PHOTO - VIBE: person walking a green field at sunset with dog)
Use CINCH yellow (#FFCC00) + black as the brand pair
Show the actual product: galvanized-steel trap studio shots on clean backgrounds
Heritage cues: Since 1909 badge, handmade-in-Oregon, Americana without kitsch
Clear mole-vs-gopher wayfinding — it is the key purchase decision
Don't reuse the current site's photos (client wants all photos replaced)
No poison/chemical imagery; no gory dead-animal photography
Avoid cartoonish pest illustrations that undercut professional-grade positioning
Don't bury the store behind educational content — Purchase Now stays primary
Avoid yellow text on white (fails contrast); yellow is a field/accent color
Lifetime warranty / free shipping on trap orders / fulfilled within 24 hours / handmade in Oregon since 1909
Don Sprague legacy woven through homepage

background_progression.band: full-bleed golden-hour photo, darkening toward bottom edge into #111111
#F6F5F0 — ONE continuous warm surface across both

page_flow.motif_continuity: The yellow tunnel-line (thin #FFCC00 line, occasionally dashed, like a mole run seen in cross-section) travels DOWN the page: exits 01-hero bottom-left under the scrim into the black strip; runs horizontally through 02-trust-strip as the tick separators baseline; exits 02 bottom-right, enters 03 top-right and dips between the two mound cards; exits 03 bottom-center, enters 04 top-center and underlines the headline; crosses the 04→05 seam unbroken (same band — the line is the only seam marker); exits 05 bottom-left, enters 06 top-left where it turns steel-silver against the dark; exits 06 bottom-right, enters 07 top-right connecting the quote cards; exits 07 bottom and terminates in 08 at the signup input as a pointed end (trap set — line ends).
rhythm_rules: At most 3 sections use the eyebrow→headline→content-below silhouette (01, 04, 06); 03 is a split comparison, 05 is a horizontal step row, 07 is a masonry quote wall, 08 is a band+footer stack
05-how-it-works and 07-testimonials are quiet: mostly negative space, headline scale at most half the hero's
02-trust-strip renders at 1536x512 and is a single short band, never a full frame
Dark sections (02, 06, footer of 08) are the page's anchors — no other section goes dark
Section heights vary: 03 and 04 are the densest; 05 and 07 breathe

hard_rules: NO star ratings, review counts, or aggregate scores anywhere — none exist for this brand (proof.json rating=null). Rendering one is an automatic QA fail.
NO dead-animal or gore imagery; no poison/chemical/bait imagery; traps are shown clean and unset or in-soil without any animal
NO cartoonish pest illustrations — if a mole/gopher appears it is photographic or a minimal stamped-line icon
Yellow #FFCC00 is NEVER text on white/light backgrounds — yellow is a field, band, or accent color; text on yellow is always black #111
Real people (Don Sprague, staff, customers) are NEVER generated — neutral no-face placeholder areas only; hero figure is distant/back-turned with no face detail
The CINCH logo/wordmark is used AS-IS — never redesigned, restyled, or re-lettered; render as plain condensed type or a neutral placeholder block
All quotes, prices, and claims verbatim from this manifest — no invented stats, no invented press, no lorem
USDA and university claims exactly as written: 'Ranked #1 by five universities' + USDA 'all-around best performer' — never embellish
Free shipping applies to TRAP ORDERS in the continental US — never render unconditional 'free shipping on everything'
No imagery from the current cinchtraps.com site — all photography direction is new (golden-hour lifestyle + clean studio product)
